articles.title: Bill Gates will testify in the Epstein probe; Pam Bondi testimony postponed
articles.summary: Bill Gates will appear before the House Oversight Committee in June. The Department of Justice said Wednesday that former Attorney General Pam Bondi will not testify for now.

articles.title: In 20 Minutes, Gilgo Beach Killer Admits to Murdering 8 Women
articles.summary: As he pleaded guilty on Wednesday, Rex Heuermann gave the same one-word answer each time he was asked how he killed one of his victims.

articles.title: White House Secures Foreign Steel for Ballroom Project
articles.summary: ArcelorMittal, a European steel maker, is donating tens of millions of dollars of foreign steel for President Trump’s new ballroom.

articles.title: Israel Says Iran Cease-Fire Does Not Extend to Lebanon and Continues Strikes
articles.summary: Deadly airstrikes pummeled Lebanon in Israel’s largest bombing wave yet in the monthlong war with Iran-backed Hezbollah.

articles.title: Has Trump Met His War Goals in Iran?
articles.summary: On the first day of the pause, Iran fired missiles and launched drones in the region. It said an oil refinery on Lavan Island had been attacked. Israel continued its strikes in Lebanon.

articles.title: Fact check: Trump's objectives in the war with Iran have largely not been achieved
articles.summary: Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.

articles.title: Trump Administration Investigating L.A. Schools’ Gender Disclosure Policies
articles.summary: The investigation into the nation’s second-largest school district was prompted by a lawsuit from parents who say the policies contributed to their child’s death.

articles.title: Bondi Won’t Appear on Capitol Hill for Scheduled Epstein Deposition
articles.summary: Pam Bondi had already been working to avoid testifying before she was fired as attorney general. The House Oversight Committee said she would not honor her subpoena because she was no longer in the po

articles.title: What Is In Iran’s 10-Point Proposal, and How Does It Compare to U.S. Demands?
articles.summary: The plan, which reasserts Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz and maintains the country’s right to nuclear enrichment, is not the same as the one President Trump said was a “workable basis” for neg

articles.title: Why OpenAI bought 'SportsCenter for Silicon Valley'
articles.summary: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.

articles.title: Greetings from downtown Cairo, where unpretentious cafés are part of centuries-old charm
articles.summary: Downtown Cairo, or Wust el-Balad as it's known, is a trove of hidden gems. Imprinted on every high-ceilinged building, arched balcony and iconic roundabout are relics that feel like love letters from

articles.title: Democrats keep doing better in elections since Trump returned to office
articles.summary: With elections in Georgia and Wisconsin Tuesday, Democrats continued to overperform, which the party started in 2025 when it regularly improved on its margins compared to the presidential race in 2024

articles.title: A humorist faces life with Stage 4 lung cancer: 'The future disappeared for me'
articles.summary: In 2020, Annabelle Gurwitch went to urgent care for a COVID-19 test and learned she had cancer. She writes about life as a "cancer slacker" in her memoir, The End of My Life is Killing Me.

articles.title: Who Is Rex Heuermann, the Man Accused in the Gilgo Beach Serial Killings?
articles.summary: Mr. Heuermann was a successful architectural consultant who lived on Long Island with his family. He was arrested in 2023.

articles.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
articles.summary: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.

articles.title: U.S., Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins House race
articles.summary: The U.S. reached a last-minute ceasefire with Iran just before Trump's deadline for the country to meet his demands. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins the U.S. House race in Georgia.

articles.title: How Trump's threats to Iran have affected the global economy
articles.summary: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Tomas {TAHM-us} Philipson, a former senior economic adviser in President Trump's first term, about the impact of the president's wartime threats on the global economy.

articles.title: A fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire shows cracks as attacks continue across the region
articles.summary: Iran and the U.S. and Israel said they would suspend strikes but countries in the region continued to report attacks and Israel said it would not stop its assault in Lebanon.

articles.title: Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization
articles.summary: President Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization and bomb its power plants by Tuesday night. Online, he said he agreed to suspend the bombing of Iran for two weeks.

articles.title: A Moscow poet highlights the challenges of speaking out against the war in Ukraine
articles.summary: In Russia, protesting against the war in Ukraine can be dangerous. But one Russian poet living in Moscow, Vadim Dzyuba, is speaking out.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
